question_title: What will be the value of "Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 30-Year Constant Maturity, Quoted on an Investment Basis, Inflation-Indexed" on 2026-02-25?
summary: The 30-Year TIPS yield (DFII30) was 2.48% on Feb 12, 2026 — the last available observation. The resolution date is Feb 25, which is 8 trading days away (accounting for Presidents' Day on Feb 16). Over the prior 3.5 months, the series has fluctuated between ~2.44 and ~2.65 with daily standard deviation of ~2.7bp and essentially zero drift. A bootstrap Monte Carlo simulation using 68 empirical daily changes produces a symmetric distribution centered near 2.48%. The recent sharp 15bp decline from 2.63 suggests possible downward pressure, but over this short horizon the random walk model dominates. I use the bootstrap simulation results directly without manual adjustment.

factors.description: Latest observed value is 2.48% (Feb 12), serving as the anchor point for the random-walk forecast
Daily volatility of ~2.7bp implies ~7.5bp std over 8 trading days — modest uncertainty band
Full-sample drift is near zero (0.015bp/day), supporting a centered distribution at current level
Recent sharp decline of 15bp over 8 days (2.63 → 2.48) could signal continued downward pressure
Series has shown mean-reverting behavior within the 2.45-2.65 range, slight upward pull possible

Let me also check for any data that may have been published after Feb 12.Now let me write my meta-reflection and provide the final forecast.**Forecast Summary for DFII30 on 2026-02-25:**

The 30-Year TIPS yield was **2.48%** on Feb 12 (latest data). Resolution is 8 trading days later on Feb 25.

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 2.38% |
| 20th | 2.42% |
| 40th | 2.46% |
| 60th | 2.50% |
| 80th | 2.54% |
| 90th | 2.58% |

**Key reasoning:**
- Daily volatility is ~2.7bp with near-zero drift over 3.5 months → random walk model applies
- Bootstrap Monte Carlo from 68 empirical daily changes gives a symmetric distribution centered at ~2.48%
- Recent 15bp decline (2.63→2.48) is notable but could revert or continue — the bootstrap captures both scenarios
- 80% confidence interval spans roughly 12bp in each direction, consistent with σ_daily × √8 ≈ 7.5bp
